title: Listening - Part 1
description: You are going to hear people talking in eight situations. For questions 1–8, choose the best answer (A, B or C).
instructions: You will hear each piece twice. For questions 1–8, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear.

extracts.context: You hear a woman leaving a message for a neighbour.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Hi Aaron, it’s Beth. Thanks again for agreeing to feed my cat while I’m away this weekend. Her food is in the cupboard beside the fridge, and she has plenty of water. ||Woman: [1]She usually comes in from the garden at about six, so could you please make sure the back door is open for her around then[/1]? I’ll be home late on Sunday.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Hi Aaron, it’s Beth. Thanks again for agreeing to feed my cat while I’m away this weekend. Her food is in the cupboard beside the fridge, and she has plenty of water.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: She usually comes in from the garden at about six, so could you please make sure the back door is open for her around then? I’ll be home late on Sunday.

questions.question: What does Beth ask Aaron to do?

options.A: buy more food for the cat
options.B: let the cat into the house in the evening
options.C: visit the cat on Sunday morning

questions.explanation: Beth asks Aaron to make sure the cat can come in from the garden at about six (evidence [1]).

extracts.context: You hear two friends talking about a cookery class.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: How was your first cookery lesson? Were you worried about making mistakes? ||Man: A bit, because I’d never cooked anything more difficult than pasta before. I thought the teacher might rush us. ||Woman: And did she? ||Man: Not at all. [2]She let us work slowly and even laughed when my bread came out in a strange shape, so the whole evening felt very relaxed[/2].

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: How was your first cookery lesson? Were you worried about making mistakes?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: A bit, because I’d never cooked anything more difficult than pasta before. I thought the teacher might rush us.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: And did she?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Not at all. She let us work slowly and even laughed when my bread came out in a strange shape, so the whole evening felt very relaxed.

questions.question: What surprised the man about the cookery lesson?
questions.explanation: He was surprised that the teacher created a relaxed, friendly atmosphere (evidence [2]).

extracts.context: You hear an announcement at a language centre.
extracts.audio_script: Guide: Students, a reminder that the language centre library will close at five today while new shelves are installed. Any books due today can be returned tomorrow without a fine. ||Guide: [3]If you need a quiet place to study this evening, classroom twelve will remain open until nine o’clock and has computers available[/3]. Please keep drinks outside the room.

dialogue.speaker: guide
dialogue.text: Students, a reminder that the language centre library will close at five today while new shelves are installed. Any books due today can be returned tomorrow without a fine.

dialogue.speaker: guide
dialogue.text: If you need a quiet place to study this evening, classroom twelve will remain open until nine o’clock and has computers available. Please keep drinks outside the room.

questions.question: What is offered to students this evening?

options.A: extra time to return library books
options.B: a place where they can work

questions.explanation: Students needing somewhere to study can use classroom twelve until nine (evidence [3]).

extracts.context: You hear a radio interview with a young app designer.
extracts.audio_script: Presenter: Adam, you designed an app that reminds people where they left things. Where did the idea come from? ||Man: My school encouraged coding, but I hadn’t thought of making an app myself. Then [4]my brother was always losing his football kit before matches, so I created a simple reminder system for him[/4]. Friends asked for it too, and the app grew from there.

dialogue.speaker: presenter
dialogue.text: Adam, you designed an app that reminds people where they left things. Where did the idea come from?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: My school encouraged coding, but I hadn’t thought of making an app myself. Then my brother was always losing his football kit before matches, so I created a simple reminder system for him. Friends asked for it too, and the app grew from there.

questions.question: What first gave Adam the idea for his app?

options.A: a problem experienced by his brother
options.B: a project he had to complete at school
options.C: a suggestion made by his friends

questions.explanation: Adam began the app to help his brother remember his football kit (evidence [4]).

extracts.context: You hear a customer returning headphones to a shop.
extracts.audio_script: Assistant: Hello. Is there a problem with those headphones? ||Customer: Yes. They’re comfortable, and the sound is good when they work, but they keep disconnecting from my phone every few minutes. ||Assistant: Would you like a refund? ||Customer: [5]I’d prefer a different pair, perhaps another brand, because I still need headphones for travelling to work[/5]. ||Assistant: Certainly. Let me show you some alternatives.

dialogue.speaker: assistant
dialogue.text: Hello. Is there a problem with those headphones?

dialogue.speaker: customer
dialogue.text: Yes. They’re comfortable, and the sound is good when they work, but they keep disconnecting from my phone every few minutes.

dialogue.speaker: assistant
dialogue.text: Would you like a refund?

dialogue.speaker: customer
dialogue.text: I’d prefer a different pair, perhaps another brand, because I still need headphones for travelling to work.

dialogue.speaker: assistant
dialogue.text: Certainly. Let me show you some alternatives.

questions.question: What does the customer want to do?
questions.explanation: The customer would like a different pair rather than a refund (evidence [5]).

extracts.context: You hear a teacher speaking to a student about a school play.
extracts.audio_script: Student: I’m worried about the school play tomorrow. I know my lines, but I still feel uncomfortable on stage. ||Teacher: You were much more confident in yesterday’s rehearsal. ||Student: Only because nobody was watching. ||Teacher: [6]Try wearing your costume for the final rehearsal this afternoon; once you feel like the character, performing in front of people may seem easier[/6]. ||Student: I’ll try that.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.text: I’m worried about the school play tomorrow. I know my lines, but I still feel uncomfortable on stage.

dialogue.speaker: teacher
dialogue.text: You were much more confident in yesterday’s rehearsal.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.text: Only because nobody was watching.

dialogue.speaker: teacher
dialogue.text: Try wearing your costume for the final rehearsal this afternoon; once you feel like the character, performing in front of people may seem easier.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.text: I’ll try that.

questions.question: What does the teacher suggest the student should do?

options.A: practise in front of an audience
options.C: rehearse in the clothes for the play

questions.explanation: The teacher recommends wearing the costume during rehearsal to build confidence (evidence [6]).

extracts.context: You hear two friends planning a day out.
extracts.audio_script: Man: The weather forecast’s changed again. It says heavy rain for Saturday, so our picnic by the lake may not be much fun. ||Woman: True. We could still take the train to Greenfield. There’s that new museum beside the station. ||Man: Good idea. [7]Let’s go there instead, and save the lake walk for a weekend when we won’t be carrying umbrellas all day[/7]. ||Woman: Agreed.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: The weather forecast’s changed again. It says heavy rain for Saturday, so our picnic by the lake may not be much fun.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: True. We could still take the train to Greenfield. There’s that new museum beside the station.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Good idea. Let’s go there instead, and save the lake walk for a weekend when we won’t be carrying umbrellas all day.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Agreed.

questions.question: What do the friends decide to do on Saturday?

options.C: stay at home until the weather improves

questions.explanation: Because of the rain, they decide to visit the museum instead of going to the lake (evidence [7]).

extracts.context: You hear a radio presenter talking about a charity run.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: This year’s Riverside Charity Run takes place next Sunday at ten o’clock, with distances for adults and children. All money raised will support the local hospital. You may collect your number on the morning of the run. ||Woman: [8]However, everyone who wants to take part must complete the online registration form by Friday evening, as entries cannot be accepted on the day[/8].

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: This year’s Riverside Charity Run takes place next Sunday at ten o’clock, with distances for adults and children. All money raised will support the local hospital. You may collect your number on the morning of the run.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: However, everyone who wants to take part must complete the online registration form by Friday evening, as entries cannot be accepted on the day.

questions.question: What must runners do before the event?

options.B: sign up on the internet in advance

questions.explanation: People must register online by Friday evening because day-of-event entries are not accepted (evidence [8]).
